claimText: Type 5 diabetes, also known as J-type diabetes, is associated with chronic undernutrition and low body weight.
evidenceExcerpt: Malnutrition-related diabetes has been officially recognized and named type 5 diabetes at International Diabetes Federation's (IDF) World Diabetes Congress. Also called J-type diabetes, is known to be associated with under nutrition and low weight.
examUse: Statement-based question on emerging or lesser-known types of diabetes.

claimText: Type 1 diabetes is characterized by the autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells.
evidenceExcerpt: Type 1: Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching diabetes types with their physiological causes.
distractorHooks: Caused by insulin resistance due to obesity

claimText: Type 2 diabetes is primarily caused by obesity and overnutrition.
evidenceExcerpt: Type 2: Mainly caused by obesity and over nutrition.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing between Type 1, Type 2, and Type 5 diabetes.
distractorHooks: Caused by autoimmune destruction of beta cells

claimText: Gestational diabetes is a condition characterized by high blood glucose levels occurring specifically during pregnancy.
evidenceExcerpt: Gestational: Occurs with high blood glucose during pregnancy.
examUse: Statement-based question on pregnancy-related health conditions.
distractorHooks: Occurs due to autoimmune destruction of beta cells
Is a permanent condition caused by childhood malnutrition
